title: DIY Laundry Basket Shelves – Free Project Plan
summary: This laundry basket shelf holds 6 basic laundry baskets.
description: This laundry basket shelf holds 6 basic laundry baskets. It is designed to be the height of a standard washer and dryer. The dimensions can easily be modified to fit any space. This is a basic build, with basic plywood and minimal tools. Perfect for a beginner.

steps.title: Cut Plywood and Drill Pocket Holes
steps.body: Using 3/4" plywood, cut the following: 3- 24" x 37 1/2" with pocket holes on short sides (vertical pieces) 2- 24" x 37 1/4" no pocket holes (top and bottom) 4- 24" x 17 1/2" with pocket holes on long sides (shelves) Using 1/4" plywood backing, cut the following: 1- 37 1/4" x 39" (backing)

steps.title: Build Box
steps.body: Using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws, attach the 37 1/2" x 24" pieces to the 37 1/4" x 24" pieces.

steps.title: Attach Middle Support
steps.body: Using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws, attach the middle vertical piece (37 1/2" x 24") to the center of the box. It should be about 17 1/2" on each side, but because materials vary, it's best to measure to find center.

steps.title: Attach Shelves
steps.body: Using wood glue and 1 1/4" pocket hole screws, attach the horizontal shelves, with the pocket holes on the underside. These shelves should be about 12" to 12 1/4" apart.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand, putty, and paint or stain your piece.

steps.title: Add Backing
steps.body: Using staples, add the backing to the back of your shelf.
